---
title: "Pre-processing and quality control"
format:
    nf-core-html: default
jupyter: python3
---

# Introduction

Write a nice summary here....

```{python}
#| tags: [parameters]
#| echo: false
input_h5ad = 'SAMN14430801_unified.h5ad SAMN14430799_unified.h5ad'

 # Input: all the sample files with space delim from .collect.

# for now, skip all parts with filtering cutofss.
samplesheet = 'samples_mouse.csv'

```


```{python}
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os
import scanpy as sc
import seaborn as sns
import anndata as ad

#from IPython.display import display, Markdown
#from textwrap import dedent
#plt.rcParams["figure.figsize"] = (6, 6)
```

```{python}
input_files = input_h5ad.split(" ")

alldata = {}
for file in input_files:
    alldata[file] = sc.read_h5ad(file)

adata = ad.concat(alldata)
```

# Quality controls

There are several different quality metrics that are normally computed for
spatial data. Common metrics include the number of genes with a least 1 count
(`n_genes_by_counts`), counts per spot (`total_counts`) as well as the
percentage of counts from mitochondrial, ribosomal and haemoglobin genes
(`pct_counts_[mt/ribo/hb]`).



## Violin plots

The following violin plots show the distribution of the number of genes per
counts and counts per spot, as well as the percentage of counts from
mitochondrial, ribosomal and haemoglobin genes:

```{python}
#| layout-nrow: 2
sc.pl.violin(adata, ['n_genes_by_counts', 'total_counts'], groupby = "batch",
    multi_panel=True, jitter=0.4, rotation= 45)

```
